sponsor_or_steward: University of Oxford, CEPI, icddr,b, Serum Institute of India
setting: Bangladesh Phase IIa trial; investigational ready reserve stored by SII
status_summary: World-first Phase II Nipah vaccine trial launched in Bangladesh; SII reserve manufacturing planned for up to 100,000 investigational doses that could be used under emergency-use conditions.
next_milestone_or_gap: Phase II safety/immunogenicity readout, continued assay/correlate development, and reserve governance for outbreak deployment.
source_titles: CEPI Phase II launch; CEPI investigational reserve | ISRCTN87634044 ChAdOx1 NipahB registry | https://www.isrctn.com/ISRCTN87634044
curation_note: Mapped above Phase II because a public investigational reserve manufacturing commitment is part of the same development path; this is not licensure.

lineage_or_scope: Bangladesh strain glycoprotein; potentially single-dose rVSV candidate
platform: Live attenuated recombinant vesicular stomatitis virus vector expressing NiV-B G and Ebola GP
sponsor_or_steward: Public Health Vaccines, CEPI, NIAID originator technology
setting: Phase II Bangladesh trial funded/planned; stockpile option if successful and approved
status_summary: CEPI announced US$17.3M to support mid-stage testing in Bangladesh after Phase Ia/Ib safety and immunogenicity; the planned study targets about 500 adults and 75 paediatric participants.
next_milestone_or_gap: Verify launch/recruitment status, then Phase II safety, tolerability, manufacturability, and immunogenicity data.
evidence_class: Phase II funded/planned; Phase I/IB complete
source_titles: CEPI PHV02 mid-stage trial announcement; PHV product page; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05178901/NCT06221813 | https://phvaccines.com/PHV02.html | https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT05178901 | https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06221813
review_flags: NCT05178901 reports COMPLETED; review whether the curated stage still describes the public pathway. | NCT06221813 reports COMPLETED; review whether the curated stage still describes the public pathway.
curation_note: Public sources checked support “Phase II funded/planned”; the dashboard does not claim a verified Phase II recruitment start.

lineage_or_scope: Hendra soluble G antigen evaluated as a Nipah vaccine; preclinical protection against NiV and HeV
platform: Recombinant soluble Hendra virus G glycoprotein subunit with alum adjuvant
sponsor_or_steward: Auro Vaccines, PATH, CEPI; original USU/HJF technology
setting: US Phase 1 completed/published; potential reactive outbreak-control use case
status_summary: Phase 1 adult study is completed and published; reported interpretation was that tested doses/regimens were safe and immunogenic, with preclinical rationale for cross-protection across Nipah and Hendra.
next_milestone_or_gap: Define dose/regimen, immune correlates, and affected-area or immune-bridging development pathway.
source_titles: CEPI first Phase 1 announcement; Lancet/PubMed phase 1 record; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04199169; npj Vaccines single-dose NHP evidence | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41248666/ | https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT04199169 | https://www.nature.com/articles/s41541-021-00284-w
curation_note: Shown under Nipah clinical development because the human trial indication is Nipah; also tagged to Hendra because the antigen and animal-protection rationale are Hendra/Nipah cross-protective.

lineage_or_scope: NiV-M chimeric pre-fusion F/G design; potential cross-reactive henipavirus approach
platform: Lipid nanoparticle mRNA encoding chimeric pre-fusion F linked to G
status_summary: A 2026 Nature Medicine Phase 1 report found the vaccine safe and well tolerated in 40 adults, with no safety-related pauses or serious adverse events reported.
next_milestone_or_gap: Larger endemic-region cohorts, dose/regimen selection, live-virus neutralization/correlate work, and development-pathway decision.
source_titles: Nature Medicine phase 1 report; NIH launch announcement; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT05398796 | https://www.nih.gov/news-events/news-releases/nih-launches-clinical-trial-mrna-nipah-virus-vaccine | https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT05398796
curation_note: Mapped to Phase 1 complete because peer-reviewed first-in-human results were publicly available at build time.

lineage_or_scope: Recombinant measles vector expressing Nipah antigens
sponsor_or_steward: University of Tokyo, European Vaccine Initiative, SCARDA; prior CEPI support
setting: Belgium first-in-human study scheduled for April 2026; Bangladesh Phase 1/2 planned
status_summary: EVI announced support for first-in-human clinical development, with a Belgium Phase 1 trial scheduled to begin in April 2026 and later Phase 1/2 studies planned in Bangladesh.
next_milestone_or_gap: Confirm first participant dosing/recruitment, then Phase 1 safety/immunogenicity readout.
source_titles: EVI first-in-human trial announcement; EU CTIS public trial entry | https://euclinicaltrials.eu/ctis-public/view/2025-522293-37-00?lang=en
curation_note: Early clinical status is based on public announcement and trial registry listing; recruitment/completion status should be rechecked before operational decisions.

lineage_or_scope: Bangladesh-strain multi-epitope G/F/N design targeted to CD40 on antigen-presenting cells
platform: Humanized anti-CD40 antibody fused to conserved Nipah epitopes
setting: African green monkey preclinical challenge model
status_summary: Cell Reports Medicine reported immunogenicity and protective efficacy of a CD40-targeted multi-epitope Nipah vaccine in African green monkeys.
next_milestone_or_gap: Translation package, manufacturability, toxicology, and decision on clinical-enabling investment.
source_titles: Cell Reports Medicine CD40.NiV; PubMed record | https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38471503/
curation_note: Included as a notable preclinical platform row; not a verified clinical development programme.

lineage_or_scope: Nipah G head domain displayed on self-assembling ferritin nanoparticles
setting: Mouse immunogenicity and Syrian hamster lethal-challenge studies
status_summary: npj Vaccines reported complete protection in hamsters and faster/broader neutralizing responses against NiV-Malaysia, NiV-Bangladesh, and Hendra pseudovirus/assays compared with soluble NiV G.
next_milestone_or_gap: Reproducibility, NHP studies, manufacturability, and clinical-enabling package if prioritized.
curation_note: Included to show preclinical breadth and Hendra cross-neutralization signal; not a clinical product.

virus: NiV, HeV, GhV, and historical MojV panel in mice
lineage_or_scope: Bivalent NiV/HeV and tetravalent G-protein Fc-fusion antigen designs
setting: Mouse immunogenicity / neutralization; not a clinical programme
status_summary: Preclinical work reported broad antibody responses with multi-antigen Fc-fusion constructs, while highlighting limited cross-protection between some henipa-like groups.
next_milestone_or_gap: Update antigen panel to current ICTV taxonomy, test against current species/provisional viruses, and define whether a pan-henipavirus target product profile is feasible.
source_titles: Viruses/PMC Fc-based recombinant henipavirus vaccines; DOAJ summary | https://doaj.org/article/8f5a7962eacc47df8cadd98fee54ced4
curation_note: Shown as broad preclinical concept. MojV has been reclassified into Parahenipavirus in current taxonomy, so this row is not counted as a core Henipavirus species pathway.

lineage_or_scope: Horse vaccination to reduce equine disease and horse-to-human spillover risk
platform: Recombinant soluble Hendra virus G glycoprotein subunit
setting: Licensed veterinary vaccine for horses in Australia
status_summary: CSIRO and partners launched Equivac HeV in November 2012; APVMA full registration was announced in August 2015. It is a veterinary vaccine, not a licensed human Hendra vaccine.
next_milestone_or_gap: Maintain uptake, update surveillance for Hendra variants, and clarify whether human Hendra indications need dedicated development.
source_titles: CSIRO Hendra vaccine history; Zoetis APVMA registration release | https://news.zoetis.com/press-releases/press-release-details/2015/Hendra-Virus-Vaccine-Now-Fully-Registered-in-Australia/default.aspx
curation_note: Placed in licensed lane because the veterinary product is licensed and deployed. It should not be interpreted as a licensed human vaccine.

lineage_or_scope: Humans occupationally exposed to horses/bats; human-label vaccine indication
platform: None publicly identified for a dedicated human Hendra label
status_summary: A veterinary horse vaccine exists; public sources checked did not identify a dedicated human Hendra vaccine indication advancing separately from cross-protective Nipah/Hendra candidates.
next_milestone_or_gap: Clarify target product profile and regulatory path if human Hendra vaccination is prioritized; evaluate whether HeV-sG-V or other cross-protective candidates can support a Hendra indication.
evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
source_titles: CSIRO Hendra vaccine history; CEPI HeV-sG-V cross-protective rationale; ICTV Henipavirus report | https://cepi.net/cepi-funded-nipah-virus-vaccine-candidate-first-reach-phase-1-clinical-trial | https://ictv.global/report/chapter/paramyxoviridae/paramyxoviridae/henipavirus
curation_note: Gap does not mean impossible or unnecessary; it means no separate human Hendra clinical path was identified from public sources.

status_summary: Current public sources identify Cedar virus as a henipavirus species, but no dedicated vaccine development pathway or clinical programme was found.
next_milestone_or_gap: Risk characterization, receptor/tropism work, animal-pathogenicity assessment, and only then a target product profile if warranted.
evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
source_titles: ICTV Henipavirus report; species list search result
curation_note: No pathway found is a source-based curation result, not proof that no unpublished work exists.

candidate: Ghanaian bat / Kumasi virus vaccine pathway
platform: None publicly identified as target-specific clinical pathway
status_summary: Ghanaian bat/Kumasi virus is in the current henipavirus species frame; public sources checked did not identify a target-specific clinical vaccine path.
next_milestone_or_gap: Risk assessment, updated antigenic panel work, and decision on inclusion in broad/pan-henipavirus vaccine concepts.
evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
source_titles: ICTV Henipavirus report; Fc-based broad vaccine concept | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7232446/
curation_note: Broad-platform data are captured separately; this row represents absence of a Ghanaian bat/Kumasi-specific clinical programme in public sources.

status_summary: Angavokely virus was added to the henipavirus species frame; no dedicated vaccine programme or clinical path was found in public sources.
next_milestone_or_gap: Risk characterization, receptor usage/pathogenicity work, and update broad-vaccine antigen panels if warranted.
evidence_class: Gap lane / no public clinical pathway found
source_titles: ICTV current species-list result; JVI/PMC discovery paper | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9517717/
curation_note: Mapped as a gap because discovery/characterization exists but no vaccine development pathway was identified.

lineage_or_scope: Pteropid bat isolate, Australia; closest to Angavokely in published analysis
platform: No vaccine candidate; receptor/tropism/risk work only
sponsor_or_steward: CSIRO and collaborators / Emerging Infectious Diseases report
setting: Australian bat surveillance and in-vitro characterization
status_summary: A 2025 Emerging Infectious Diseases report described Salt Gully virus as a novel henipavirus from Australian pteropid bats, most closely related to Angavokely virus, with unknown human-disease risk.
next_milestone_or_gap: Establish taxonomy status, receptor usage, host range, animal pathogenicity, and whether countermeasure inclusion is warranted.
source_titles: Emerging Infectious Diseases Salt Gully virus report
curation_note: Included as a surveillance/gap lane rather than a core recognized species pathway.
